preamble: The shift from the voiced alveolar stop [d] to the voiceless dental fricative [θ] is a two-step process involving spirantization (becoming a fricative) and devoicing. This typically occurs in word-final or syllable-coda positions where consonants are prone to weakening and losing their voicing.

examples.note: Word-final /d/ devoices to [θ] in central and northern Spain; the intermediate stage is voiced [ð].
